supplements.name: Omega-3 (EPA/DHA)
supplements.description: Essential fatty acids that support brain function, heart health, and reduce inflammation. EPA and DHA are the active forms found in fish oil.

pairWith.reason: Both are fat-soluble and absorb better together with dietary fat
Vitamin E may help prevent oxidation of omega-3 fatty acids

supplements.evidenceSummary: Strong evidence for cardiovascular benefits and cognitive support. Meta-analyses show consistent benefits for triglyceride reduction and anti-inflammatory effects.

citations.title: Omega-3 Fatty Acids and Cardiovascular Disease: Effects on Risk Factors, Molecular Pathways, and Clinical Events
Omega-3 fatty acids and cognitive decline in the elderly
Vitamin D and omega-3 fatty acid supplementation in combination
Vitamin E and omega-3 fatty acids: effectors of bone health

supplements.name: Vitamin D3
supplements.description: Essential vitamin produced by sun exposure, crucial for bone health, immune function, and mood regulation. Most adults are deficient.

pairWith.reason: K2 helps direct calcium mobilized by D3 to bones rather than arteries
Magnesium is required for vitamin D metabolism and activation

supplements.evidenceSummary: Strong evidence for bone health, immune function, and deficiency-related mood improvements. Widespread deficiency makes supplementation beneficial for most adults.

citations.title: Vitamin D and the Immune System
Vitamin D supplementation: effect on outcomes in pregnancy
Vitamins D and K as pleiotropic nutrients
Synergy of vitamin D and K for bone and cardiovascular health
Magnesium status and supplementation influence vitamin D status

supplements.name: Magnesium Glycinate
supplements.description: Highly bioavailable form of magnesium bound to glycine. Supports sleep, muscle relaxation, stress response, and over 300 enzymatic reactions.

pairWith.reason: Magnesium is required for vitamin D metabolism
B6 may enhance magnesium uptake into cells

separateFrom.reason: High doses of zinc can interfere with magnesium absorption
High calcium can compete with magnesium for absorption

supplements.evidenceSummary: Strong evidence for sleep quality improvement and stress reduction. Glycinate form is well-tolerated and may have additional calming effects from glycine.

citations.title: The effect of magnesium supplementation on primary insomnia in elderly
Magnesium in Prevention and Therapy
Magnesium status and supplementation influence vitamin D status
Magnesium and vitamin B6 supplementation for stress
Mineral interactions affecting nutrient absorption
Calcium-magnesium interaction in absorption

supplements.name: Vitamin B12
supplements.description: Essential vitamin for nerve function, DNA synthesis, and red blood cell formation. Deficiency is common in vegetarians, vegans, and older adults.

pairWith.reason: B12 and folate work together in methylation pathways

separateFrom.reason: High-dose vitamin C may reduce B12 absorption

supplements.evidenceSummary: Essential for vegans and vegetarians who cannot obtain adequate B12 from diet. Strong evidence for preventing deficiency-related neurological issues.

citations.title: Vitamin B12 deficiency in vegetarians and vegans
Vitamin B12 status and rate of brain volume loss in older adults
Folate and vitamin B12 metabolic interaction
Effect of vitamin C on vitamin B12 absorption

dietRelevance.vegetarian: Recommended as dietary B12 is limited without meat
dietRelevance.vegan: Essential as plant foods contain no reliable B12 source

supplements.name: Vitamin C
supplements.description: Powerful antioxidant that supports immune function, collagen synthesis, and iron absorption. Water-soluble vitamin that needs regular intake.

pairWith.reason: Vitamin C significantly enhances non-heme iron absorption
Vitamin C regenerates vitamin E, enhancing antioxidant protection

separateFrom.reason: High doses may reduce B12 absorption

supplements.evidenceSummary: Strong evidence for immune support and antioxidant function. Modest evidence for reducing cold duration when taken regularly.

citations.title: Vitamin C and Immune Function
Vitamin C for preventing and treating the common cold
The enhancing effect of ascorbic acid on iron absorption
Interaction between vitamins C and E in human erythrocytes
Effect of vitamin C on vitamin B12 availability

supplements.name: Zinc
supplements.description: Essential mineral for immune function, wound healing, protein synthesis, and enzymatic reactions. Important for testosterone production.

separateFrom.reason: Zinc and iron compete for absorption when taken together
High zinc intake can deplete copper over time
High doses can interfere with each other's absorption

supplements.evidenceSummary: Strong evidence for immune function and modest evidence for reducing cold duration when taken early. Essential for those with inadequate dietary intake.

citations.title: Zinc in Human Health: Effect of Zinc on Immune Cells
Zinc lozenges and the common cold: a meta-analysis
Competitive inhibition of iron absorption by zinc
Zinc-induced copper deficiency
Mineral nutrient interactions

supplements.name: Creatine Monohydrate
supplements.description: One of the most researched sports supplements. Supports muscle strength, power output, and may have cognitive benefits.

pairWith.reason: Insulin spike from carbs may enhance creatine uptake into muscles

separateFrom.reason: Caffeine may reduce creatine's ergogenic benefits, though evidence is mixed

supplements.evidenceSummary: Extremely well-researched with strong evidence for muscle strength and power. Emerging evidence for cognitive benefits, especially during sleep deprivation.

citations.title: International Society of Sports Nutrition position stand: creatine supplementation
Effects of creatine supplementation on cognitive function
Carbohydrate ingestion augments skeletal muscle creatine accumulation
Caffeine and creatine interaction

supplements.name: Melatonin
supplements.description: Natural hormone that regulates sleep-wake cycles. Useful for jet lag, shift work, and sleep onset issues. Start with lowest effective dose.

pairWith.reason: Both support relaxation and sleep quality through different mechanisms

avoidWith.reason: Caffeine suppresses natural melatonin production and counteracts effects

supplements.evidenceSummary: Strong evidence for jet lag and circadian rhythm disorders. Moderate evidence for general sleep onset. Lower doses (0.5-1mg) often as effective as higher doses.

citations.title: Meta-analysis: melatonin for the treatment of primary sleep disorders
Melatonin for jet lag
Magnesium and melatonin in the management of sleep disorders
Caffeine effects on sleep and melatonin

supplements.cautionNote: Use the lowest effective dose. Not for long-term daily use without clinical guidance. May cause morning grogginess at higher doses.

supplements.name: Iron
supplements.description: Essential mineral for oxygen transport in blood. Deficiency causes fatigue and anemia. Only supplement if deficient—excess iron is harmful.

avoidWith.reason: Tannins and polyphenols reduce iron absorption by up to 60%

supplements.evidenceSummary: Only beneficial for those with documented deficiency. Unnecessary supplementation can cause oxidative stress. Women of reproductive age and vegetarians/vegans at higher risk of deficiency.

citations.title: Iron deficiency anemia: assessment, prevention, and control
Enhancing effect of ascorbic acid on iron absorption
Calcium inhibits iron absorption
Iron absorption and tea consumption
Competitive inhibition of iron absorption by zinc

supplements.cautionNote: Only supplement if you have confirmed iron deficiency. Excess iron causes oxidative damage. Consult a clinician and test levels before supplementing.

dietRelevance.vegetarian: Higher risk of deficiency; plant iron is less bioavailable
dietRelevance.vegan: Significantly higher risk of deficiency; consider testing levels

supplements.name: Ashwagandha
supplements.description: Adaptogenic herb traditionally used in Ayurveda. May help reduce cortisol, support stress resilience, and improve sleep quality.
supplements.evidenceSummary: Moderate evidence for stress and cortisol reduction. Some evidence for improved sleep and reduced anxiety. Effects may take several weeks to manifest.

citations.title: A prospective, randomized double-blind, placebo-controlled study of safety and efficacy of ashwagandha root extract
Efficacy and safety of ashwagandha root extract in insomnia and anxiety
Effects of ashwagandha on cortisol and sleep quality

supplements.cautionNote: May interact with thyroid medications. Not recommended during pregnancy. Discontinue 2 weeks before surgery.

supplements.name: Vitamin K2 (MK-7)
supplements.description: Fat-soluble vitamin that directs calcium to bones and teeth while preventing arterial calcification. MK-7 form has longer half-life.

pairWith.reason: K2 ensures calcium mobilized by D3 goes to bones, not arteries
K2 helps direct calcium to appropriate tissues

supplements.evidenceSummary: Strong evidence for bone health when combined with vitamin D. Emerging evidence for cardiovascular protection through reduced arterial calcification.

citations.title: Vitamin K2 and bone health
High dietary menaquinone intake is associated with reduced coronary calcification
Vitamins D and K as pleiotropic nutrients
Vitamin D and vitamin K status in combined supplementation
Vitamin K-dependent carboxylation of osteocalcin

supplements.cautionNote: If taking blood thinners (warfarin/coumadin), consult your doctor—K2 affects clotting factors.

supplements.name: Magnesium Citrate
supplements.description: Well-absorbed form of magnesium. Supports muscle function, energy production, and bowel regularity. More likely to cause loose stools than glycinate.

pairWith.reason: Magnesium is required for vitamin D metabolism

separateFrom.reason: High doses of zinc can interfere with magnesium absorption
High calcium can compete with magnesium for absorption

supplements.evidenceSummary: Good bioavailability and well-studied. May have mild laxative effect at higher doses, which some find beneficial for regularity.

citations.title: Bioavailability of magnesium citrate vs oxide
Magnesium status and supplementation influence vitamin D status
Mineral nutrient interactions
Calcium-magnesium interaction in absorption

supplements.name: L-Theanine
supplements.description: Amino acid found in tea that promotes relaxation without sedation. Often paired with caffeine for focused calm.

pairWith.reason: L-theanine smooths caffeine's stimulatory effects, reducing jitters while maintaining focus

supplements.evidenceSummary: Good evidence for promoting relaxation and focus when combined with caffeine. May help with sleep quality when taken in evening.

citations.title: L-theanine, a natural constituent in tea, and its effect on mental state
The combined effects of L-theanine and caffeine on cognitive performance
L-theanine and caffeine in combination affect human cognition

supplements.name: Rhodiola Rosea
supplements.description: Adaptogenic herb that may help with physical and mental fatigue. Traditionally used for stress resilience and endurance.
supplements.evidenceSummary: Moderate evidence for reducing fatigue and improving stress resilience. May be mildly stimulating, so morning use is preferred.

citations.title: Rhodiola rosea for physical and mental fatigue: a systematic review
Clinical efficacy of Rhodiola rosea extract in stress-related fatigue

supplements.cautionNote: May have mild stimulating effects. Avoid taking late in the day. Not recommended for those with bipolar disorder.

supplements.name: Coenzyme Q10 (CoQ10)
supplements.description: Antioxidant naturally produced by the body that supports cellular energy production. Levels decline with age and statin use.

pairWith.reason: Both are fat-soluble and support cardiovascular health

supplements.evidenceSummary: Good evidence for supporting mitochondrial function and reducing statin-related muscle symptoms. May support cardiovascular health.

citations.title: Coenzyme Q10 supplementation in aging and disease
Effect of CoQ10 on statin-associated myopathy
Combined supplementation with CoQ10 and omega-3
